title: Haydn's Symphony Dazzles London Audience
story: Music filled the air as Joseph Haydn's 99th Symphony in E made its grand debut in London, England. title: Stargazer-in-Chief Takes the Helm
story: Edmond Halley, the brilliant astronomer who predicted the return of a famous comet, stepped into a stellar new role on February 10, 1720. He became the second Astronomer Royal at the Greenwich Observatory in England. This prestigious position put Halley in charge of mapping the stars and guiding important scientific work.

story: Pow! Canadian boxer Tommy Burns showed his strength in London. On February 10, 1908, he knocked out English fighter Jack Palmer in just four rounds. story: Splash! A gold ring plunged into the Baltic Sea. In 1920, General Jozef Haller celebrated Poland's newfound access to the open sea with a unique ceremony. title: A Banker's Secret Fight Against Evil
story: Walraven van Hall wasn't your average banker. Born in Amsterdam on February 10, 1906, he grew up to become a hero. title: A Friar's Mission of Peace
story: A champion for refugees was born on February 10, 1910. Dominique Pire, a Belgian Dominican friar, dedicated his life to helping those displaced by World War II.
